checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill value 697377036.16 end 2009-01-31 declining to 148639290.11 end 2013-11-02
evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet, trend of intangibles amortizing down over time

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K cash flow statement, FY2012 - OCF consistently exceeds NI across all years, which is not the concerning direction for overstatement risk

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit value 1245136379.76 vs Revenues value 2082339782.44 (period 2010-01-31 to 2010-05-01), gross margin ~59.8%
Revenues value 2266452246.07 and CostOfGoodsSold value 1131687938.08 (period 2013-08-04 to 2013-11-02), implied gross margin ~50.1%
evidence.location: 10-Q income statement - margin decline tracks the concurrent revenue decline, not a divergence

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss value -271268164.75 for quarter 2009-02-01 to 2009-05-02, then value 490659094.95 for quarter 2009-08-02 to 2009-10-31
evidence.location: 10-K quarterly net income table - shows large swings including a loss quarter, consistent with (not smoother than) revenue seasonality

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: 10-K balance sheet note, FY2010 year-end allowance
10-K balance sheet note, FY2011 year-end allowance - a 64% decline

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent value 1118119297.10 end 2011-01-29 vs 991211737.10 end 2012-01-28 (only ~11% decline)
evidence.location: 10-K balance sheet - allowance fell far faster than the receivable base or revenue (which fell only ~6%)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date 2007-07-13 immediately following 10-K filing_date 2007-07-02 and 10-Q/A filing_date 2007-07-02
evidence.location: filing_chronology - cluster of four separate non-timely (NT) notifications over ~9 months followed by amended 10-K and 10-Q, consistent with a restatement episode (e.g., stock-option expensing/backdating review common in 2006-2007)

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual XBRL data provided continuously from FY2008 (end 2009-01-31) through Q3 FY2013 (end 2013-11-02), spanning ~5 years across Revenues, AR, AP, Inventory, NetIncomeLoss, cash flow statement items, and allowance/reserve disclosures

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Likely understatement of stock-based/options compensation expense in periods prior to 2007 (consistent with the industry-wide options backdating review wave), later corrected via restated financials. Evidenced by a cluster of NT 10-Q, NT 10-Q/A, NT 10-K, and NT 10-Q filings between Sept 2006 and June 2007, followed almost immediately by a 10-K/A and same-day 10-Q/A in July 2007.

rationale_evidence.quote: NT 10-Q filing_date 2006-09-07; NT 10-Q/A filing_date 2006-09-28; NT 10-Q filing_date 2006-12-06; NT 10-K filing_date 2007-03-29; NT 10-Q filing_date 2007-06-08; 10-K filing_date 2007-07-02; 10-Q/A filing_date 2007-07-02; 10-K/A filing_date 2007-07-13

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction of the bad-debt allowance/reserve by ~64% in FY2011 (from $17.9M to $6.5M) while gross receivables and revenue fell only modestly, potentially reflecting a reserve release that understates credit risk and modestly overstates net receivables/earnings quality.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=17927642.96 (2011-01-29) declining to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=6481307.79 (2012-01-28), while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent only fell from 1118119297.10 (2011-01-29) to 991211737.10 (2012-01-28)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Accounts receivable grew ~60.6% (from $540.6M to $868.4M) even as revenue declined ~4.8% (from $7,181.2M to $6,833.5M) between FY2008 and FY2009 year-ends, suggesting either a change in receivable composition (e.g., credit-card/other receivables reclassified onto the balance sheet) or a slowing of collections/timing shift in revenue recognition that outpaced actual sales activity.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=540557620.07 (2009-01-31) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=868383287.83 (2010-01-30); Revenues=7181189247.85 (FY2008) vs Revenues=6833452360.01 (FY2009)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-K balance sheet and income statement comparatives

top_signals: Cluster of four NT (late-filing) notifications between Sept 2006 and June 2007 followed by a 10-K/A and same-day 10-Q/A in July 2007, indicating a likely historical restatement episode

Allowance for doubtful accounts fell ~64% (17.9M→6.5M) between FY2010 and FY2011 year-ends while receivables/revenue declined only modestly, inconsistent with normal reserve-to-receivable relationships
Accounts receivable grew ~60.6% while revenue declined ~4.8% between FY2008 and FY2009 year-ends, a marked divergence from the pattern seen in other years where AR tracked or fell faster than revenue
